description: Letter from Meʾir b. ʿEli ha-Kohen, in Damascus (חדרך סוריא), to his brother Ṭoviyya b. ʿEli, in Fustat. Dating: beginning of September 1127 CE. The letters deals with financial matters and the purchase of flax and indicates that the Gaon (Maṣliaḥ) was by then in Egypt. "Mutual affection was expressed by kissing the eyes" (Goitein, Med. Soc., viii, C, 2, n. 117). Cf. T-S 10J17.8. Also mentions someone who got very sick (balagha kathīr) but God sent health (recto, 8–10). Mentions the same Yaḥyā b. Najm who appears in T-S 6J2.13.
tags: Mazliah Gaon, Illness, Damascus, Toviya b. Eli, taqbil, illness letter 969-1517
description_he: מכתב ממאיר בן אלי הכהן, בדמשק, לאחיו טוביה בן אלי בפוסטאט, תחילת ספטמבר 1127. עסקים ורכישת פשתן.
